articles.title: Israel heavily bombs southern city of Tyre in Lebanon
articles.summary: Israel heavily bombs southern city of Tyre in Lebanon Israeli strikes on the southern Lebanese city of Tyre, also known as Sour in Arabic, over the past hours have been some of the most intense bombardment of the area since March, according to Lebanse media. L'Orient Today reported that buildings in the city centre were wholly destroyed, wounding multiple people. The publication reported that Israelis forces bombed the same area in the centre of Tyre on Sunday shortly after an initial strike.

articles.title: Netanyahu describes Israeli seizure of Lebanon castle as 'shift in policy'
articles.summary: Netanyahu describes Israeli seizure of Lebanon castle as 'shift in policy' Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu has described Israeli forces' capture of Beaufort castle in southern Lebanon as a "dramatic shift" in Israel's war on Lebanon. "Today, we have returned to Beaufort in a different way. We have returned united, determined, and stronger than ever," Netanyahu said in a video statement. "The capture of Beaufort is a dramatic stage and a dramatic shift in the policy we are leading. We have broken the barrier of fear. articles.title: How Poland Became Europe’s Growth Leader
articles.summary: Poland has become one of Europe’s standout growth stories, with an economy that crossed $1 trillion and a new generation of entrepreneurs returning home after studying and working abroad. Finance Minister Andrzej Domański points to decades of reform, EU integration, foreign investment and a diversified economy as drivers of Poland’s rise, while venture capitalist Aleksandra Pedraszewska says Warsaw’s technology scene now offers opportunities that were far less obvious a decade ago. But economists warn that sustaining the boom will require Poland to address large deficits, elevated defense spen

articles.title: Three offshore platforms reportedly resume production at Iran's South Pars gas field
articles.summary: Three offshore platforms reportedly resume production at Iran's South Pars gas field Iran has restored gas production at three offshore platforms in the South Pars gas field that had been forced to halt output after Israeli attacks disrupted processing capacity at some onshore facilities, the chief executive of the Pars Oil and Gas Company told Iranian state media on Sunday. Touraj Dehqani said the platforms had not been damaged. He said production from the three platforms was being routed to other processing plants in the region while repairs continue at damaged facilities, including the

articles.title: Israeli strike near hospital in southern Lebanon wounds 13 medics
articles.summary: Israeli strike near hospital in southern Lebanon wounds 13 medics An Israeli strike near a hospital in Tyre, south Lebanon has wounded 13 staffers, the Lebanese health ministry said. "The Israeli enemy launched an airstrike in the vicinity of Hiram Hospital in Tyre, injuring 13 hospital staff members and causing significant damage," the ministry said in a statement.  It urged "the international community to put an end to the escalating and expanding Israeli attacks".

The capture of the medieval castle and ridge deepens Israeli footprint in the country beyond the demarcation zone known as the "Yellow Line", which it established in April following the ceasefire. articles.title: Iran's Ghalibaf says no US deal without tangible results
articles.summary: Iran's Ghalibaf says no US deal without tangible results Iran will not accept any agreement ending its conflict with the US unless there is certainty that the Iranian people's rights are secured, top negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf said on Sunday according to Iranian state media. "There is no trust in the enemy's words and promises. Our only criterion is to achieve tangible results before we fulfill our commitments in return," he added after taking an oath as the re-elected speaker of parliament.

articles.title: Hezbollah drone strike kills Israeli soldier, says army
articles.summary: Hezbollah drone strike kills Israeli soldier, says army The Israeli army announced Sunday that one of its soldiers had been killed the previous day by a Hezbollah explosive drone in southern Lebanon, bringing the number of Israeli military deaths since early March to 25. Michael Tyukin, 21, "fell in combat in southern Lebanon," the army said in a statement. An army spokesman told AFP he was killed by a Hezbollah drone strike. In total, 25 Israelis have been killed - 24 soldiers and one civilian contractor - since fighting between Israel and Hezbollah resumed on 2 March.

articles.title: IRGC arrests two suspects accused of aiding Israel, Tasnim reports
articles.summary: IRGC arrests two suspects accused of aiding Israel, Tasnim reports Iran’s Tasnim News Agency reported that the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps arrested two people in Urmia accused of collaborating with Israel. According to the report, the suspects allegedly shared coordinates and addresses of locations, including schools, mosques and Basij facilities through Telegram. Tasnim said the two also photographed sites after they were bombed and sent images of the damage to what it described as the “Zionist enemy”.
